data.title: Tickets created during sprint disappear — ticket deps shows 0 children despite successful ticket create calls
data.description:
## Description
During sprint execution for epic f5b6-775a, tasks created via ticket create with --parent=3b72-0aaf are not persisted. ticket deps shows 0 children despite multiple successful ticket create calls that returned valid IDs. Subsequently, ticket show on those IDs fails with "has no CREATE or SNAPSHOT event".

## Reproduction
1. Run ticket create task "test" --parent=3b72-0aaf --priority=1
2. Observe: returns a ticket ID (e.g., cb91-9eb3)
3. Run ticket show cb91-9eb3 → ERROR: "has no CREATE or SNAPSHOT event"
4. Run ticket deps 3b72-0aaf → children: [] (empty)

## Impact
Sprint orchestrator cannot create implementation tasks for stories, blocking batch execution entirely. The sprint has committed code but cannot track which tasks are done.

## Workaround
Unknown. Tasks were created multiple times with different IDs but none persisted.
